title: Fix duplicate paper trade entry — DB-level atomic dedup + NULL return handling
objective: Two bot_core processes are running simultaneously. Both execute the bridge independently. Both read open_slugs from fetch_open_trades(), both see the target slug as NOT open, both pass all gates, both call insert_open_trade() within milliseconds of each other. No DB constraint prevents both writes from succeeding. Result: duplicate open trades for the same slug with identical prices. This has been observed with 6 separate duplicate pairs across today's log and historical 10/3, 11/3 capacity violations from April 4th. The fix: make insert_open_trade() atomic at the DB level so that the second writer always loses, regardless of how many processes are racing.

duplicate_events_today: 15:23:13 — trade=55 and trade=56, mlb-phi-col BUY_NO @0.6532, 4ms apart
16:16:54 — trade=57 and trade=58, mlb-cin-tex BUY_YES @0.7236, 33ms apart
16:34:33 — trade=60 and trade=61, mlb-mia-nyy BUY_YES @0.2713, 67ms apart
18:25:22 — trade=99 and trade=100, mlb-sea-laa BUY_NO @0.4121, 52ms apart
18:29:02 — trade=101 and trade=102, mlb-stl-det BUY_YES @0.4321, <5ms apart
18:31:31 — trade=103 and trade=104, mlb-sea-laa BUY_YES @0.7839, <5ms apart
confirmed_evidence.capacity_violations_april4: BRIDGE SKIP at 10/3 and 11/3 — chronic compounding from prior restarts
confirmed_evidence.two_process_signature: Paired duplicate log entries (BRIDGE SKIP, BRIDGE OPEN) consistently appear within 1-70ms throughout the log, confirming two bot_core processes with slightly offset loop timers
confirmed_evidence.identical_prices: Each duplicate pair has exactly the same price — both processes fetched the same orderbook snapshot within the same market state window

deliverables.label: Add UNIQUE partial index and atomic check-before-insert in core/db.py

change_1.where: _CREATE_SQL — after the existing CREATE INDEX statements
change_1.what:
Add a partial UNIQUE index that prevents two open rows for the same slug:

  CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_trades_one_open_per_slug
  ON trades(market_slug) WHERE status='open';

This is the hard DB-level guarantee. Even if two processes both pass all application checks, only one INSERT can succeed. The second raises sqlite3.IntegrityError.
change_1.note: SQLite partial indexes (WHERE clause) are supported since SQLite 3.8.9. The index applies only to rows where status='open', so closing a trade does not violate the constraint — multiple closed rows for the same slug are allowed.

change_2.what:
Wrap the insert in a BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction + pre-check, and catch IntegrityError:

  def insert_open_trade(trade: Trade, sport: str = 'unknown') -> int | None:
      with _db_conn('insert_open_trade') as conn:
          conn.execute('BEGIN IMMEDIATE')  # exclusive write lock — prevents concurrent read-check-write races
          existing = conn.execute(
              'SELECT id FROM trades WHERE market_slug=? AND status=\'open\' LIMIT 1',
              (trade.market_slug,)
          ).fetchone()
          if existing:
              logger.warning(
                  'Duplicate open rejected: slug=%s already open id=%s',
                  trade.market_slug, existing[0]
              )
              return None
          try:
              c = conn.cursor()
              c.execute('INSERT INTO trades ...', (...))
              conn.commit()
              row_id = c.lastrowid
              logger.info('Opened trade id=%d %s @ %.4f', row_id, trade.side, trade.entry_px)
              return row_id
          except sqlite3.IntegrityError as e:
              logger.warning('Duplicate insert blocked by constraint: slug=%s err=%s', trade.market_slug, e)
              return None

BEGIN IMMEDIATE acquires an exclusive write lock at the start of the transaction. The check and the insert happen within the same lock, so no other SQLite writer can interleave. The IntegrityError catch is a final safety net for the UNIQUE index.
change_2.note: The function return type changes from int to int | None. Callers must handle None.

deliverables.label: Handle None return from insert_open_trade in bot_core.py — native loop
deliverables.where: Native signal loop, around line 426 — after: trade = open_position(...)
deliverables.what:
Currently:
  trade = open_position(market, sig, ob, mode=mode_ctx.mode)
  trade_id = insert_open_trade(trade, sport=SPORT)
  trade.id = trade_id
  open_count += 1
  open_per_market[market.market_id] = open_per_market.get(market.market_id, 0) + 1

Change to:
  trade = open_position(market, sig, ob, mode=mode_ctx.mode)
  trade_id = insert_open_trade(trade, sport=SPORT)
  if trade_id is None:
      logger.info('OPEN SKIPPED (duplicate slug) slug=%s', market.slug)
      continue
  trade.id = trade_id
  open_count += 1
  open_per_market[market.market_id] = open_per_market.get(market.market_id, 0) + 1

deliverables.label: Handle None return from insert_open_trade in bot_core.py — bridge section
deliverables.where: Bridge section, around line 484 — after: trade = open_position(..., source='model_bridge')
deliverables.what:
Currently:
  trade = open_position(market, signal, ob, mode=mode_ctx.mode, source='model_bridge')
  trade_id = insert_open_trade(trade, sport=SPORT)
  trade.id = trade_id
  logger.info('BRIDGE OPEN trade=%d ...', trade_id, ...)

Change to:
  trade = open_position(market, signal, ob, mode=mode_ctx.mode, source='model_bridge')
  trade_id = insert_open_trade(trade, sport=SPORT)
  if trade_id is None:
      logger.info('BRIDGE OPEN SKIPPED (duplicate slug) slug=%s', market.slug)
      continue
  trade.id = trade_id
  logger.info('BRIDGE OPEN trade=%d ...', trade_id, ...)

acceptance_criteria: insert_open_trade() returns None instead of crashing when a duplicate open slug is attempted
insert_open_trade() uses BEGIN IMMEDIATE to make the check-and-insert atomic across concurrent processes
UNIQUE partial index exists on trades(market_slug) WHERE status='open'
Native loop logs 'OPEN SKIPPED (duplicate slug)' and continues when insert returns None
Bridge section logs 'BRIDGE OPEN SKIPPED (duplicate slug)' and continues when insert returns None
Two bot_core processes racing on the same slug: exactly 1 trade written to DB, second blocked at insert
Closing a trade (status='closed') still allows future open trades for the same slug — partial index allows this
migration_note: The CREATE UNIQUE INDEX will fail if duplicate open rows already exist in the DB. Before adding the index, close or void the extra duplicate rows. The simplest migration: after the index creation attempt fails (IntegrityError), log a warning and skip — the BEGIN IMMEDIATE check will still protect against new duplicates even if the index doesn't exist yet. Alternatively, add explicit dedup logic in init_db() to detect and close extras before attempting the index.
verification: Read core/db.py — confirm BEGIN IMMEDIATE is present in insert_open_trade()
Read core/db.py — confirm IntegrityError is caught and returns None
Read core/db.py — confirm UNIQUE INDEX line is in _CREATE_SQL
Read bot_core.py — confirm None check after insert_open_trade in native loop
Read bot_core.py — confirm None check after insert_open_trade in bridge section
Trace: two concurrent calls to insert_open_trade(slug='mlb-sea-laa-...') → first returns int, second returns None
rollback: Revert core/db.py and bot_core.py. The UNIQUE INDEX, if created in the DB, is harmless — it can be dropped with 'DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_trades_one_open_per_slug' if needed. Existing duplicate rows in the DB are not affected by rollback.
